Note

License reminder: this project is distributed under the MIT License. See the repository LICENSE file. Disclaimer: this simulator is provided as-is for research, calibration, and educational use. Users assume all risks, including any direct or indirect damage, data loss, hardware damage, injury, or regulatory non-compliance arising from use or misuse.

User Interface

src.ui.main_window

Main GUI Window Module

src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets

Accessible Widgets Module

Main GUI Window Module

Main application window for BLDC motor control simulator.

Provides comprehensive GUI for: - Motor parameter configuration - Load profile definition - Control and monitoring - Real-time plotting - Data export

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

0.8.1

src.ui.main_window.QAccessible

alias of _DummyQAccessible

class src.ui.main_window.AccessibleTextBlock(parameter_name, unit, index, total_count, parent=None)[source]

Bases: QFrame

Accessible visual text block component for monitoring values. Each block displays a labeled monitoring value with units. Optimized for screen reader detection and visual distinction.

__init__(parameter_name, unit, index, total_count, parent=None)[source]
update_value(value)[source]

Update the displayed value.

keyPressEvent(event)[source]

Handle keyboard navigation for accessibility.

class src.ui.main_window.SimulationThread[source]

Bases: QThread

Background simulation thread.

update_signal

pyqtSignal(*types, name: str = …, revision: int = …, arguments: Sequence = …) -> PYQT_SIGNAL

types is normally a sequence of individual types. Each type is either a type object or a string that is the name of a C++ type. Alternatively each type could itself be a sequence of types each describing a different overloaded signal. name is the optional C++ name of the signal. If it is not specified then the name of the class attribute that is bound to the signal is used. revision is the optional revision of the signal that is exported to QML. If it is not specified then 0 is used. arguments is the optional sequence of the names of the signal’s arguments.

finished_signal

pyqtSignal(*types, name: str = …, revision: int = …, arguments: Sequence = …) -> PYQT_SIGNAL

types is normally a sequence of individual types. Each type is either a type object or a string that is the name of a C++ type. Alternatively each type could itself be a sequence of types each describing a different overloaded signal. name is the optional C++ name of the signal. If it is not specified then the name of the class attribute that is bound to the signal is used. revision is the optional revision of the signal that is exported to QML. If it is not specified then 0 is used. arguments is the optional sequence of the names of the signal’s arguments.

__init__()[source]
engine: SimulationEngine | None
svm: SVMGenerator | None
controller: BaseController | None
get_latest_state()[source]

Return the latest simulation snapshot without blocking control loop.

Return type:

dict

set_simulation(engine, svm, controller, max_duration=0.0, pwm_frequency_hz=None)[source]

Assign engine, svm and controller before running thread.

start_simulation()[source]

Flag thread to begin executing the simulation loop.

stop_simulation()[source]

Stop simulation loop.

run()[source]

Main simulation loop.

class src.ui.main_window.CurrentSpectrumWindow(window_size_samples=512, parent=None)[source]

Bases: QMainWindow

Dedicated FFT window for controller-facing current harmonics.

closed

pyqtSignal(*types, name: str = …, revision: int = …, arguments: Sequence = …) -> PYQT_SIGNAL

types is normally a sequence of individual types. Each type is either a type object or a string that is the name of a C++ type. Alternatively each type could itself be a sequence of types each describing a different overloaded signal. name is the optional C++ name of the signal. If it is not specified then the name of the class attribute that is bound to the signal is used. revision is the optional revision of the signal that is exported to QML. If it is not specified then 0 is used. arguments is the optional sequence of the names of the signal’s arguments.

__init__(window_size_samples=512, parent=None)[source]
set_window_size(samples)[source]

Update FFT window length and preserve newest samples when possible.

Return type:

None

push_snapshot(snapshot)[source]

Consume latest simulation snapshot without blocking control loop.

Return type:

None

apply_display_settings(grid_enabled, mag_x_scale, mag_y_scale, phase_x_scale, phase_y_scale, amplitude_mode, phase_unit)[source]

Apply FFT display configuration from parent GUI controls.

Return type:

None

closeEvent(event)[source]
Return type:

None

class src.ui.main_window.BLDCMotorControlGUI[source]

Bases: QMainWindow

Main BLDC Motor Control GUI

Comprehensive application for BLDC motor simulation and control.

Features: - Screen reader accessible - Real-time motor simulation - V/f speed control - Data logging and export - Visualization tools

__init__()[source]
motor: BLDCMotor | None
engine: SimulationEngine | None
svm: SVMGenerator | None
controller: VFController | None
sim_thread: SimulationThread | None
calib_process: QProcess | None
calib_output_path: Path | None
current_fft_window: CurrentSpectrumWindow | None
get_simulation_params_info()[source]

Return a string with current dt and motor time constants (for testing).

Return type:

str

closeEvent(event)[source]

Override closeEvent to ensure all background processes are properly terminated. This prevents orphaned processes when the GUI is closed.

Return type:

None

Accessible Widgets Module

Provides accessible PyQt6 widgets with proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation.

All widgets support: - Screen reader accessibility - Tab key navigation - Clear keyboard shortcuts - Descriptive labels and help text

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

0.8.0

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleDoubleSpinBox(label='', min_val=0.0, max_val=100.0, initial=0.0, step=0.1, decimals=3, suffix='')[source]

Bases: QDoubleSpinBox

Accessible double precision spinner with screen reader support.

Features: - Auto-generated ARIA labels - Clear status text for screen readers - Keyboard-friendly increment/decrement

__init__(label='', min_val=0.0, max_val=100.0, initial=0.0, step=0.1, decimals=3, suffix='')[source]

Initialize accessible spinner.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Descriptive label for screen readers

  • min_val (float) – Minimum value

  • max_val (float) – Maximum value

  • initial (float) – Initial value

  • step (float) – Step size for increment/decrement

  • decimals (int) – Decimal places

  • suffix (str) – Unit suffix (e.g., “ V”, “ A”)

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleSpinBox(label='', min_val=0, max_val=100, initial=0, step=1, suffix='')[source]

Bases: QSpinBox

Accessible integer spinner for screen readers.

__init__(label='', min_val=0, max_val=100, initial=0, step=1, suffix='')[source]

Initialize accessible integer spinner.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Descriptive label

  • min_val (int) – Minimum value

  • max_val (int) – Maximum value

  • initial (int) – Initial value

  • step (int) – Step size

  • suffix (str) – Unit suffix

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleComboBox(label='', items=None)[source]

Bases: QComboBox

Accessible combo box with screen reader support.

__init__(label='', items=None)[source]

Initialize accessible combo box.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Field label

  • items (list) – List of items to add

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleButton(text, tooltip='')[source]

Bases: QPushButton

Accessible button with clear labeling.

__init__(text, tooltip='')[source]

Initialize accessible button.

Parameters:

  • text (str) – Button text

  • tooltip (str) – Tooltip text (shown to screen readers)

keyPressEvent(event)[source]

Handle keyboard activation.

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleGroupBox(title='', description='')[source]

Bases: QGroupBox

Accessible group box for organizing related controls.

__init__(title='', description='')[source]

Initialize accessible group box.

Parameters:

  • title (str) – Group title

  • description (str) – Description for screen readers

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleTabWidget[source]

Bases: QTabWidget

Accessible tab widget with keyboard navigation.

__init__()[source]

Initialize accessible tab widget.

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.LabeledSpinBox(label, min_val=0.0, max_val=100.0, initial=0.0, step=0.1, decimals=3, suffix='', description='')[source]

Bases: QWidget

Convenience widget combining a label and spinner.

Simplifies accessibility by grouping related elements.

valueChanged

pyqtSignal(*types, name: str = …, revision: int = …, arguments: Sequence = …) -> PYQT_SIGNAL

types is normally a sequence of individual types. Each type is either a type object or a string that is the name of a C++ type. Alternatively each type could itself be a sequence of types each describing a different overloaded signal. name is the optional C++ name of the signal. If it is not specified then the name of the class attribute that is bound to the signal is used. revision is the optional revision of the signal that is exported to QML. If it is not specified then 0 is used. arguments is the optional sequence of the names of the signal’s arguments.

__init__(label, min_val=0.0, max_val=100.0, initial=0.0, step=0.1, decimals=3, suffix='', description='')[source]

Initialize labeled spinner.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Field label

  • min_val (float) – Minimum value

  • max_val (float) – Maximum value

  • initial (float) – Initial value

  • step (float) – Step size

  • decimals (int) – Decimal places

  • suffix (str) – Unit suffix

  • description (str) – Accessibility description

value()[source]

Get current value.

Return type:

float

setValue(val)[source]

Set value.

Return type:

None

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.LabeledComboBox(label, items=None, description='')[source]

Bases: QWidget

Convenience widget combining label and combo box.

currentTextChanged

pyqtSignal(*types, name: str = …, revision: int = …, arguments: Sequence = …) -> PYQT_SIGNAL

types is normally a sequence of individual types. Each type is either a type object or a string that is the name of a C++ type. Alternatively each type could itself be a sequence of types each describing a different overloaded signal. name is the optional C++ name of the signal. If it is not specified then the name of the class attribute that is bound to the signal is used. revision is the optional revision of the signal that is exported to QML. If it is not specified then 0 is used. arguments is the optional sequence of the names of the signal’s arguments.

__init__(label, items=None, description='')[source]

Initialize labeled combo box.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Field label

  • items (list) – List of items

  • description (str) – Accessibility description

currentText()[source]

Get selected text.

Return type:

str

setCurrentText(text)[source]

Set selected text.

Return type:

None

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleTableWidget(label='', description='')[source]

Bases: QTableWidget

Accessible table widget with screen reader support and keyboard navigation.

Features: - ARIA labels for screen readers - Arrow key navigation (Up, Down, Left, Right) - Space/Enter for selection - Tab key moves to next table - Announces current cell to screen readers

__init__(label='', description='')[source]

Initialize accessible table widget.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Accessible name for the table

  • description (str) – Description for screen readers

keyPressEvent(event)[source]

Handle keyboard navigation.

class src.ui.widgets.accessible_widgets.AccessibleListWidget(label='', description='')[source]

Bases: QListWidget

Accessible list widget with screen reader support and keyboard navigation.

Features: - ARIA labels for screen readers - Arrow key navigation - Space/Enter for selection - Announces list position to screen readers

__init__(label='', description='')[source]

Initialize accessible list widget.

Parameters:

  • label (str) – Accessible name for the list

  • description (str) – Description for screen readers

keyPressEvent(event)[source]

Handle keyboard navigation.
